ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Jan-10-2022 Continued To Mar-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Gwen Wu, via ZOOM, for Angelique McGuire, Beneficiary; Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, for Minna Fernan, successor Trustee; Robert Cowan, via ZOOM, for Tiana Wilbert, Beneficiary.

Mr. Schultz reports that counsel have met via video and that they have made progress in resolving this matter and that an appraisal has been obtained which may help in the next round of settlement discussions. Mr. Cowan suggests setting a future status date and that he is working on an informal accounting which should be done by the end of this week and which he anticipates will assist in settling the matter. Mr. Schultz agrees.

After additional discussion, the matters on calendar are continued to Mar-14-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody (via ZOOM). Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Cristobal. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Jan-10-2022 Continued To Mar-14-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Filing Of Proof Of Insurance
Off Calendar Oct-25-2021 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Continued To Jan-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Oct-04-2021 Continued To Jan-10-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Buggy, via ZOOM, for Angelique McGuire, Petitioner; Andrew Schultz, via ZOOM, for Minna Fernan, via ZOOM, Successor Trustee; Robert Cowan, in person, for Tianna Wilbur, in person, Interested Party.

Mr. Schultz begins by advising the court that he just learned that Mr. Cowan is representing Ms. Wilbur. He then reports that two mediations have taken place and currently the principal issue is the property taxes owed to the city which total $105,000. Mr. Schultz then offers oral testimony regarding the current state of the real property and that there is much work to be done before they can be rented. Mr. Schultz also reports that the property is currently uninsured. Regardless of the mounting obstacles, Mr. Schultz realizes that the family would like to keep the property and advises that he is ready to work with the parties to keep the property. Mr. Cowan reports that his client and her brother are working to secure a loan to pay for the outstanding property taxes and repairs to the property. Ms. Schultz then confirms that the mediator is willing to continue to work with the parties.

After additional discussion, the Status of Accounting and Status of Mediation are continued to Jan-10-2022 at 9:00 am in Department 204. A Status on Proof of Insurance is set for Oct-25-2021 at 9:00 am in Department 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Of Mediation
Off Calendar Aug-09-2021 Continued To Oct-04-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Apr-19-2021 Continued To Aug-09-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa, Esq. for and with Minna Fernan, Trustee (appearing separately by video conference); Kevin Buggy, Esq., counsel for Angelique McGuire, Petitioner, Beneficiary (appearing by video conference) Wesley Bookman and Deborah Bookman, former Co-Trustees in pro per (present in the Courtroom); Tiana Wilbert, beneficiary, in pro per (present in the Courtroom).

At the Court's inquiry, Mr. Siracusa, Ms. Bookman, Mr. Bookman and Ms. Wilbert all consent to pro bono mediation and as such, the Court refers the matter to pro bono mediation.
After further discussion, the Court continues the current matter and sets hearing on the status of mediation for August 9, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Tauscher. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Mar-22-2021 Continued To Apr-19-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Kevin Buggy appeared by zoom for Angelique McGuire (Petitioner).
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Minna Fernan (Successor Trustee) by zoom.

The Court asked where things stand. Mr. Schultz stated that he and his Client met with Debra and Wesley Bookman, that it was a productive meeting and that it doesn't sound like the Bookmans are going to be able to get Counsel. Mr. Schultz also stated that there was a pay-on-death account for the Granddaughter, that the only asset of the trust is the house, that there are $100,000.00 in property taxes and that there are three Beneficiaries. In addition, Mr. Schultz stated that the solution would be to sell the house, that Debra's main goal is to keep the property in the family, that Debra is ill and is concerned about her housing, and that it sounds like Mr. Buggy's Client wants to keep the house and her goal is to move into the house with her Child. Furthermore, Mr. Schultz stated that there are some options they could explore, but it would require the family coming together, that he would like to get the entire family with a Mediator, that there is no mortgage on the house, requested a referral to pro bono mediation and suggested a 90-day continuance for status. Mr. Buggy stated that he hasn't had the opportunity to speak with his Client regarding mediation, but it is her goal to keep the house in the family, and that he is happy to speak with his Client, but he is hesitant to sign her up for mediation because he does not know if his Client is ready to be before the family. Status on filing of former co-trustees account is continued to April 19, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for a possible referral to mediation.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Jan-25-2021 Continued To Mar-22-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sil Vossler appeared by zoom for Angelique McGuire.
Andrew Schultz appeared by zoom for and with Minna Fernan (Trustee) by zoom.

The Court asked if there are any developments. Mr. Schultz stated that his Client reached out to the Bookmans, that the Bookmans initially were resistant to meet with them, and that a meeting was scheduled for last Friday, but Ms. Bookman was hospitalized. Mr. Schultz also stated that Asian Outreach declined to take the case, but he believes the Attorney was able to guide them (the Bookmans), that they have spoken with Mr. Bookman and that Mr. Bookman is willing to meet with them and walk them through the property, but he wants to meet when Ms. Bookman is out of the hospital. Mr. Vossler stated that this has had to be re-set twice now, that his Client has been waiting for seven years, that it is frustrating, that he is glad to hear there is progress and that Kevin Buggy will be stepping in for him. The Court raised the issue of a forensic accounting. Mr. Schultz requested a 60-day continuance and stated that he believes his Client can provide some guidance with the accounting. Status on filing of former co-trustees accounting is continued to March 22, 2021 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ORDER OF STATUS HEARING
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Dec-21-2020 Continued To Jan-25-21 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Sil Vossler, Esq., counsel for Angelique McGuire, Petitioner (appearing by video conference); Andrew Schultz, Esq. for and with Minna Fernan, Trustee (appearing separately by video conference); Wesley Bookman and Deborah Bookman, former Co-Trustees in pro per (present in the Courtroom).

Mr. Schultz reports on the status of his efforts and that of the successor trustee to contact the Bookmans to facilitate a transfer of records and assist with all of the accountings that were not filed during the Bookman's tenure as Co-trustees.

Mr. Bookman (son) and Ms. Bookman (Mother) report that they have never received any notice that they were removed as trustees and have always appeared at hearings only to be told that the matter was put over. The Bookmans report that their legal counsel declined to participate in these proceedings as not within the scope of her expertise and that her and their efforts to find suitable counsel have been unavailing at this time.

The Court reports the on various hearings and the results of the same. At the Court's invitation, Mr. Vossler provides a condensed rendition of the history of the litigation. After the Bookmans and Ms. Fernan, the Successor Trustee, exchange contact information, the Court admonishes the Bookmans to work with Ms. Fernan to complete all required accountings and strongly advises them to secure legal counsel should they continue to contend that their removal was not proper.

The Court continues the current matter and sets hearing on the status of the trust administration for January 25, 2021 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody; Clerk: R. Michael Diles; Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. Reporter: Not Reported. (D204)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On The Filing Of Former Co-Trustees Account
Off Calendar Nov-23-2020 Continued To Dec-21-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances via Zoom Video Conference:
Sil Liapis Vossler for Angelique McGuire.
Avid Alavi on behalf of Andrew Schultz for Minna Fernan.

Ms. Alavi stated that her client has not heard from the former trustees, that her client tried to contact the Bookmans, has knocked on their door with no response, was hoping they would appear today, and that they will provide 24-hours notice with a locksmith to change the locks and leave a set of keys for them. Mr. Liapis Vossler stated that representation from his client is Ms. Bookman may have terminal cancer, that the Bookmans are not interested in cooperating with Ms. McGuire and called her greedy. The Court noted the former co-trustees have not filed nor appeared, and for counsel to show some sensitivity if one of the parties has cancer.

An Order to Appear will be issued to the Former Co-Trustees to appear on December 21, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why the accounting has not been filed. The status hearing is also continued to that date.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: S. Le. Probate Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

Angelique Mcguire'S Petition For Instructions And For Removal Of Trustees, Appointment Of A Receiver Or Temporary Trustee, An Accounting, To Surcharge
Off Calendar Sep-29-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sil Liapis appeared by zoom for and with Angelique McGuire (Petitioner) by zoom.

The Court noted that no one was making any objections either in Court or remotely, and that it does not have a proposed order. Mr. Liapis stated that he is not aware of any objections, that he dropped off the order to the Court after the last hearing and that he will drop off another one today to the Probate Examiner's attention. Mr. Liapis also stated that Minna Fernan (Professional Fiduciary) has agreed to step in and that it is his understanding that the Trustees embezzled all of the assets in the probate estate. The Petition for Instructions and for Removal of Trustees, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Probate Examiner: Helen Trowbridge. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

payments.description: PETN AND OPP RE INTERNAL AFFAIRS OF 17200 TRUST
